description: Give the Studio's explicitly advertised sixty-nine paint colors bounded scenery behavior

replacements.new:
<ROUTINE ROOM-DENSITY-STUDIO-PAINT-F ()
    <SETG ROOM-DENSITY-STUDIO-SEEN T>
    <COND (<VERB? EXAMINE SEARCH LOOK-ON>
           <TELL "The paint covers walls, floor, and door in sixty-nine distinguishable colors, including several that should probably remain unnamed." CR>)
          (<VERB? RUB>
           <TELL "The paint is thoroughly dry. Texture varies from thin stains to ridges left by loaded brushes." CR>)
          (<VERB? SMELL>
           <TELL "Only a faint trace of old oil and pigment remains." CR>)
          (<VERB? KNOCK STRIKE>
           <TELL "The painted surface answers according to the solid wall or floor beneath it." CR>)
          (<VERB? TAKE MOVE PUSH>
           <TELL "You cannot remove the paint as a useful, independent object." CR>)
          (T
           <TELL "The sixty-nine colors improve the studio's commitment to excess, not its inventory." CR>)>
    <RTRUE>>

<ROUTINE ROOM-DENSITY-STUDIO-FLOOR-F ()
